program.name: Macquarie Graduate Program (Australia)
program.duration_text: Commencing February 2027 (dates are subject to change).

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian citizen, Australian permanent resident or New Zealand citizen to be eligible for Macquarie’s Graduate and Internship Programs.
eligibility.graduation_window: Students in their final year of study or who have completed their studies within 12 months of applying (12 months from your last subject or unit).
disciplines: All degree backgrounds (Macquarie says there is a place for you no matter what you studied and they welcome students from all degree backgrounds).
minimum_requirements: Be in your final year of study, or have completed your studies within the last 12 months (measured from your last subject/unit).
Be an Australian citizen, Australian permanent resident, or New Zealand citizen.

stages.name: Step 1 - Apply
stages.details: Apply online during the application opening dates. You submit details about yourself and your CV. You receive an automatic email confirming your application was received, and the Graduate Recruitment Team reviews your application.

stages.name: Step 2 - Psychometric assessment
stages.details: If you are successful after the application review, you are invited to complete a psychometric assessment (measuring cognitive ability and behavioural style). It is usually completed from home and typically takes about an hour. There is no minimum score required to progress, and you may be asked to complete a verification assessment later.

stages.name: Step 3 - Video interview
stages.details: If Macquarie sees a strong match, you are invited to complete a video interview. You answer pre-recorded questions under timed conditions tailored to the business group you applied for.

stages.name: Step 4 - Superday / further interview/s
stages.details: If you are successful after the video interview, you may be invited to a Superday with further interviews. This can include a case study, a problem-solving activity, or an article to review and present your views on. You can also expect competency-based questions. Graduate Programme candidates may be invited to a final interview after the Superday. You will also be asked to complete a verification of your psychometric assessment at this stage.

stages.name: Step 5 - Offer
stages.details: If you are successful, a Graduate Recruiter contacts you with an offer. Macquarie says you will have five days to accept your offer.

stages.name: Step 6 - Reference checking and employment screening
stages.details: If you receive an offer, you must complete reference checks and employment screening before you commence.

tips: Check the job description for programme-specific closing dates (Macquarie notes closing dates depend on the programme).
You do not need to include a cover letter, transcripts, or references when you submit your application (these may be requested later if you are successful).
Use only the practice psychometric assessment link sent by your recruiter, as Macquarie says this is the only authentic practice assessment for their process.

exec_themes: Diversified income mix across “annuity-style” and “markets-facing” activities (balancing more stable earnings with market-dependent earnings).
Disciplined capital allocation, including prioritising higher risk-adjusted return opportunities and being willing to divest non-core businesses.
Growth and realisations in asset management (raising capital for infrastructure/energy-transition themes and monetising mature investments).
Energy transition and climate-related investing and advisory (renewables, batteries, hydrogen, sustainable aviation fuel, and related solutions).
Operational efficiency and digital-first banking execution in Australia (simplifying fees, speed of rate changes, and fraud/scam protection investments).
commercial_context.profit_engine: Macquarie’s profit model is to earn money from a mix of (1) steadier, recurring “annuity-style” businesses (notably asset management and Australian banking) and (2) more market-dependent “markets-facing” activities (notably commodities and global markets), supported by active capital allocation and occasional asset realisations (selling investments after value creation).
commercial_context.headwinds: Macquarie flagged that profits in its Commodities and Global Markets business were down due to more subdued conditions in global energy and certain commodity markets (for FY25).
commercial_context.esg: Macquarie describes ESG as part of its responsibility to stakeholders and structures its sustainability approach around environmental and social risk management, supporting climate solutions, sustainability in its own operations, people, clients/customers, community, and business conduct/ethics. It also states commitments for its own operations including sourcing the equivalent of 100% renewable electricity for global offices/data centres and maintaining net zero for Scope 1 and 2 emissions, plus supplier and travel-related (Scope 3) targets and diverse supplier spend from FY2026–FY2030.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Recent emphasis (as described by the Chair) has been on tighter/ongoing capital allocation discipline: prioritising the most promising opportunities and being willing to divest businesses that are no longer central to strategy or may perform better under alternative ownership.

notes: Application open and close dates for the Graduate Program (Australia) are listed on the 'Graduates and Interns' page for Australia.
The program start timing is stated as 'Commencing February 2027', with a note that dates are subject to change.
Work rights and graduate eligibility timing are stated on the Australia 'Graduates and Interns' page.
The page also states Macquarie considers/welcomes applications from all degree backgrounds.

Recruitment steps are listed as a numbered process on the 'Recruitment process' page (Apply → Psychometric assessment → Video interview → Superday/further interviews → Offer → Reference checking and screening).
The same recruitment-process page includes timing and content notes for several stages (for example, the assessment typically takes about an hour, and offers are held for five days).
The 'no cover letter/transcripts/references required at application' tip is from the FAQs page.

sources.title: Graduates and Interns | Recruitment Process | Macquarie Group

notes: This summary is based on a limited set of official Macquarie pages that were accessible in this run (interim result landing page, AGM update article, and sustainability pages).

Some items (especially ESG) come from high-level sustainability pages and commitments statements; more detailed metrics likely sit in the Group Net Zero and Climate Risk Report and/or full sustainability reporting not extracted here.
“Recent pivot” is expressed as an increased/ongoing strategic emphasis rather than a single discrete pivot event with a dated program name.
